title: Indefinite probabilities in quantum spacetime: A deepening of unpredictability
authors: Vittorio D'Esposito, Giuseppe Fabiano, Domenico Frattulillo
abstract: Non-commutative spacetime and quantum groups have been argued to capture non-classical features of spacetime and its symmetries in the low-energy limit of quantum gravity. In this letter, we show that employing the $SU_q(2)$ quantum group to describe rotational symmetry for spin-$\frac{1}{2}$ systems and Stern-Gerlach apparatuses leads to the description of probabilities of outcomes of spin measurements in terms of non-commuting operators. As a result, we obtain an uncertainty principle between different probability operators, realizing a notion of indefinite probabilities. This is then reflected in the non-commutativity of the entries of the rotation matrix relating the reference frames of two observers, hence fundamentally preventing them from sharply measuring their relative orientation.
summary: This paper explores the concept of 'indefinite probabilities' arising from a non-commutative spacetime geometry. It shows that in a quantum-deformed universe, the probabilities of measurement outcomes do not commute, leading to a fundamental uncertainty in determining the relative orientation of different observers.
main_problem: Investigating how non-commutative spacetime, modeled via the SUq(2) quantum group, leads to 'indefinite probabilities' where measurement outcome probabilities are non-commuting operators.
main_result: The authors derive an uncertainty principle for probability operators and rotation matrix elements, demonstrating that observers in a quantum-deformed spacetime cannot sharply measure their relative orientation.
method: The study employs the algebraic framework of Doubly Quantum Mechanics (DQM) and braiding formalism using the R-matrix to compute commutators of probability operators in a leading-order expansion of the deformation parameter.
model_or_system: A theoretical model of two observers (Alice and Bob) using spin-1/2 systems and Stern-Gerlach apparatuses within a non-commutative spacetime geometry described by the SUq(2) quantum group.
key_observables: Probability operators for spin outcomes (P(up) and P(down)) and rotation matrix elements (Rij) relating observer reference frames.
important_parameters: The deformation parameter q, which is related to the Planck length and cosmological constant, and the limit q -> 1 which recovers standard physics.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the SUq(2) quantum group as an effective description of spacetime and relies on a leading-order expansion in (1-q).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of non-commutative spacetime, presents the SUq(2) and DQM mathematical framework, derives the non-commutativity of probability operators via braiding, and concludes with the physical implications for observer orientation and uncertainty.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
